name: cvlayer
documentation:
# cvlayer

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cvlayer?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/cvlayer/)
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/cvlayer?style=flat-square)
[![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/license/osom8979/cvlayer?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/osom8979/cvlayer/)

OpenCV Layer Helper

## Overview

When performing image-based machine learning/inference, many situations arise that require pre-/post-processing appropriate for the domain environment.

A commonly used library today is [OpenCV](https://opencv.org/).

Image processing with [OpenCV](https://opencv.org/) requires insight into the image and the domain.

However, gaining these insights often requires doing something far from the essence. (Visualization, parameter modification, library conflicts, assertions, etc...)

Here are some problems that occurred to me:

...

## Features

...

## Installation

Install `cvlayer`:
```shell
pip install cvlayer
```

Install `cvlayer` with `opencv-python`:
```shell
pip install cvlayer[opencv]
```

Install `cvlayer` with `opencv-python-headless`:
```shell
pip install cvlayer[headless]
```

## Usage

### CvLayer

Just inherit `cvlayer.CvLayer`.

```python
from cvlayer import CvLayer


class YourApp(CvLayer):
    def func(self, image):
        self.cvl_cvt_color_bgr2hsv(image)
```

### CvWindow

Just inherit `cvlayer.CvWindow`.

```python
from cvlayer import CvWindow
from numpy.typing import NDArray


class YourApp(CvWindow):
    def on_frame(self, image: NDArray) -> NDArray:
        # TODO: Implement image processing logic ...
        return image


YourApp("/path/to/video/file.mp4").run()
```

## Example

The following sample is a [Perspective Transform](https://docs.opencv.org/4.x/da/d6e/tutorial_py_geometric_transformations.html) example:

```python
from sys import argv, stderr
from sys import exit as sys_exit
from typing import List, Optional

from cvlayer import CvLayer, CvMixin, CvWindow
from cvlayer.typing import PointI
from cvlayer.palette.basic import RED
from numpy.typing import NDArray


class YourApp(CvWindow, CvMixin, CvLayer):
    _points: List[PointI]

    def __init__(self, source: str, destination: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(source, destination)
        left_top = 930, 2750
        left_bottom = 846, 3098
        right_top = 1091, 2750
        right_bottom = 1361, 3098
        self._points = [left_top, left_bottom, right_top, right_bottom]
        self._scale = 2, 4

    def on_frame(self, image: NDArray) -> NDArray:
        with self.layer("select-roi") as layer:
            self.roi = layer.param("roi").build_select_roi().value
            layer.frame = layer.prev_frame

        with self.layer("perspective-points") as layer:
            points = layer.param("pp").build_select_points(self._points).value
            canvas = layer.prev_frame.copy()
            for p in points:
                self.cvl_draw_point(canvas, p, color=RED)
            layer.frame = canvas

        with self.layer("perspective-transform") as layer:
            sw = layer.param("scale-width").build_uint(self._scale[0]).value
            sh = layer.param("scale-height").build_uint(self._scale[1]).value

            xs = list(map(lambda point: point[0], self._points))
            ys = list(map(lambda point: point[1], self._points))
            x1, x2 = min(xs), max(xs)
            y1, y2 = min(ys), max(ys)
            width, height = abs(x2 - x1) * sw, abs(y2 - y1) * sh
            roi = 0, 0, width, height
            m = self.cvl_get_perspective_transform_with_quadrilateral(
                left_top=points[0],
                left_bottom=points[1],
                right_top=points[2],
                right_bottom=points[3],
                destination_roi=roi,
            )
            layer.frame = self.cvl_warp_perspective(image, m, (width, height))

        with self.layer("hsv") as layer:
            layer.frame = hsv = self.cvl_cvt_color_bgr2hsv(layer.prev_frame)
        with self.layer("hsv-h") as layer:
            layer.frame = h = hsv[:, :, 0]
        with self.layer("hsv-s") as layer:
            layer.frame = s = hsv[:, :, 1]
        with self.layer("hsv-v") as layer:
            layer.frame = v = hsv[:, :, 2]

        assert h is not None
        assert s is not None
        assert v is not None

        self.cvm_gaussian_blur("v-blur", (3, 19), 0.0, 7.0)
        self.cvm_threshold_binary("v-thresh", 230)

        return self.last_frame


def test_main(*args) -> None:
    source = args[1]
    destination = args[2] if len(args) >= 3 else None

    try:
        app = YourApp(source, destination)
        app.run()
    except Exception as e:
        print(e, file=stderr)
        sys_exit(1)
    else:
        sys_exit(0)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_main(*argv)
```

See the [LICENSE](./LICENSE) file for details. In summary,

functions.name: cvlayer.cv.threshold.adaptive_threshold
documentation:
Constant subtracted from the mean or weighted mean (see the details below).
Normally, it is positive but may be zero or negative as well.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.threshold.ThresholdResult.computed_threshold_value
documentation: The computed threshold value if Otsu's or Triangle methods used.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.types.distance_transform_label.DistanceTransformLabel.CCOMP
documentation:
Each connected component of zeros in src (as well as all the non-zero pixels
closest to the connected component) will be assigned the same label

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.types.distance_transform_label.DistanceTransformLabel.PIXEL
documentation: Each zero pixel (and all the non-zero pixels closest to it) gets its own label

classes.name: cvlayer.cv.types.distance_type.DistanceType
documentation: Distance types for Distance Transform and M-estimators

classes.name: cvlayer.cv.types.ddepth.DesiredDepth
documentation: desired depth of the destination image

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.stitcher_mode_index
documentation:
Scenario for stitcher operation.

PANORAMA
    Mode for creating photo panoramas. Expects images under perspective
    transformation and projects resulting pano to sphere.
    - `cv2.detail.BestOf2NearestMatcher`
    - `cv2.SphericalWarper`
SCANS
    Mode for composing scans. Expects images under affine transformation does
    not compensate exposure by default.
    - `cv2.detail.AffineBestOf2NearestMatcher`
    - `cv2.AffineWarper`

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.features_finder_index
documentation: Type of features used for images matching.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.matcher_index
documentation: Matcher used for pairwise image matching.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.estimator_index
documentation: Type of estimator used for transformation estimation.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.match_conf
documentation:
Confidence for feature matching step.

The recommended default values are:
- ORB is 0.3
- other feature types is 0.65

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.conf_thresh
documentation: Threshold for two images are from the same panorama confidence.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.ba_refine_mask
documentation:
Set refinement mask for bundle adjustment.

It looks like 'x_xxx', where 'x' means refine respective parameter and '_' means
don't refine, and has the following format:<fx><skew><ppx><aspect><ppy>. The default
mask is 'xxxxx'. If bundle adjustment doesn't support estimation of selected
parameter then the respective flag is ignored.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.compose_mega_pixel
documentation: Resolution for compositing step. Use -1 for original resolution.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.expos_comp_nr_filtering
documentation: Number of filtering iterations of the exposure compensation gains.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.expos_comp_block_size
documentation: BLock size in pixels used by the exposure compensator.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.range_width
documentation: Uses range_width to limit number of images to match with.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.stitching.props.StitcherProps.use_cuda
documentation: Try to use CUDA. The default value is no. All default values are for CPU mode.

functions.name: cvlayer.cv.color.saturate
documentation: dst(x, y) = saturate(src(x, y) + (src(x, y) - 128) * a)

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.WARP_INVERSE_MAP
documentation: `WARP_INVERSE_MAP` that sets `M` as the inverse transformation (`dst -> src`)

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.LU
documentation: Gaussian elimination with the optimal pivot element chosen

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.SVD
documentation:
singular value decomposition (SVD) method;
the system can be over-defined and/or the matrix src1 can be singular

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.EIG
documentation:
eigenvalue decomposition;
the matrix src1 must be symmetrical

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.CHOLESKY
documentation:
Cholesky LLT factorization;
the matrix src1 must be symmetrical and positively defined

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.QR
documentation:
QR factorization;
the system can be over-defined and/or the matrix src1 can be singular

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.perspective.MatrixDecomposition.NORMAL
documentation:
while all the previous flags are mutually exclusive,
this flag can be used together with any of the previous;

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.edge.scharr.DEFAULT_SCALE
cvlayer.cv.filter.edge.scharr.DEFAULT_DELTA
documentation: optional delta value that is added to the results prior to storing them in dst.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.edge.laplacian.DEFAULT_KERNEL_SIZE
documentation:
Aperture size used to compute the second-derivative filters.
See getDerivKernels for details.
The size must be positive and odd.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.edge.laplacian.DEFAULT_SCALE
documentation:
scale factor for the computed Laplacian values.
By default, no scaling is applied.
See getDerivKernels for details.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.edge.laplacian.DEFAULT_DELTA
documentation: delta value that is added to the results prior to storing them in dst.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_writer.VideoWriterProperty.QUALITY
documentation:
Current quality (0..100%) of the encoded videostream.
Can be adjusted dynamically in some codecs.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_writer.VideoWriterProperty.FRAMEBYTES
documentation:
(Read-only): Size of just encoded video frame.
Note that the encoding order may be different from representation order.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_writer.VideoWriterProperty.NSTRIPES
documentation: Number of stripes for parallel encoding. -1 for auto detection.

functions.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.denoising.nimd.fast_nl_means_denoising
documentation: Works with a single grayscale images.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.denoising.nimd.DEFAULT_H
documentation:
parameter deciding filter strength.
Higher h value removes noise better, but removes details of image also.
(10 is ok)

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.denoising.nimd.DEFAULT_H_FOR_COLOR_COMPONENTS
documentation: same as h, but for color images only. (normally same as h)

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.blur.bilateral.DEFAULT_BILATERAL_FILTER_D
documentation:
Diameter of each pixel neighborhood that is used during filtering.
If it is non-positive, it is computed from sigmaSpace.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.blur.bilateral.DEFAULT_BILATERAL_FILTER_SIGMA_COLOR
documentation:
Filter sigma in the color space.
A larger value of the parameter means that farther colors within the pixel neighborhood
(see sigmaSpace) will be mixed together,
resulting in larger areas of semi-equal color.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.blur.bilateral.DEFAULT_BILATERAL_FILTER_SIGMA_SPACE
documentation:
Filter sigma in the coordinate space.
A larger value of the parameter means that farther pixels will influence each other as
long as their colors are close enough (see sigmaColor ).
When d>0, it specifies the neighborhood size regardless of sigmaSpace.
Otherwise, d is proportional to sigmaSpace.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.blur.gaussian.DEFAULT_GAUSSIAN_BLUR_SIGMA_X
documentation: Gaussian kernel standard deviation in X direction.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.filter.blur.gaussian.DEFAULT_GAUSSIAN_BLUR_SIGMA_Y
cvlayer.cv.transform.flood_fill.FLOODFILL_FIXED_RANGE
documentation:
If set, the difference between the current pixel and seed pixel is considered.
Otherwise, the difference between neighbor pixels is considered
(that is, the range is floating).

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.transform.flood_fill.FLOODFILL_MASK_ONLY
documentation:
If set, the function does not change the image (newVal is ignored), and
only fills the mask with the value specified in bits 8-16 of flags as described above.
This option only make sense in function variants that have the mask parameter.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.transform.grab_cut.GrabCutMode.INIT_WITH_RECT
documentation:
The function initializes the state and the mask using the provided rectangle.
After that it runs iterCount iterations of the algorithm.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.transform.grab_cut.GrabCutMode.INIT_WITH_MASK
documentation:
The function initializes the state using the provided mask.
Note that GC_INIT_WITH_RECT and GC_INIT_WITH_MASK can be combined.
Then, all the pixels outside of the ROI are automatically initialized with GC_BGD.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.transform.grab_cut.GrabCutMode.EVAL
documentation: The value means that the algorithm should just resume.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.transform.grab_cut.GrabCutMode.EVAL_FREEZE_MODEL
documentation:
The value means that the algorithm should just run the grabCut algorithm
(a single iteration) with the fixed model.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.term_criteria.TermCriteriaType.COUNT
documentation: the maximum number of iterations or elements to compute

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.term_criteria.TermCriteriaType.EPS
documentation: the desired accuracy or change in parameters at which the iterative algorithm stops

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.term_criteria.TermCriteria.type
documentation: The type of termination criteria: COUNT, EPS or COUNT + EPS

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_capture.CaptureDomain.AVFOUNDATION
documentation: OS X Lion will have the same API

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_capture.CaptureDomain.WINRT
documentation: Microsoft Windows Runtime using Media Foundation.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_capture.CaptureDomain.OPENNI2_ASUS
documentation: for Asus Xtion and Occipital Structure sensors

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.video_capture.CaptureDomain.FFMPEG
documentation: Open and record video file or stream using the FFMPEG

functions.name: cvlayer.cv.hough_lines.hough_lines
documentation:
Finds lines in a binary image using the standard Hough transform.

```
param image

8-bit, single-channel binary source image.
The image may be modified by the function.

param rho

Distance resolution of the accumulator in pixels.

param theta

Angle resolution of the accumulator in radians.

param threshold

Accumulator threshold parameter.
Only those lines are returned that get enough votes ( >threshold ).

param srn

For the multiscale Hough transform,
it is a divisor for the distance resolution rho.
The coarse accumulator distance resolution is rho and the accurate accumulator
resolution is rho/srn. If both srn=0 and stn=0, the classical Hough transform
is used. Otherwise, both these parameters should be positive.

param stn

For the multiscale Hough transform,
it is a divisor for the distance resolution theta.

param min_theta

For standard and multiscale Hough transform,
minimum angle to check for lines. Must fall between 0 and max_theta.

param max_theta

For standard and multiscale Hough transform,
an upper bound for the angle. Must fall between min_theta and CV_PI.
The actual maximum angle in the accumulator may be slightly less than max_theta,
depending on the parameters min_theta and theta.

return

Output vector of lines.
Each line is represented by a 2 or 3 element vector (ρ,θ) or (ρ,θ,votes),
where ρ is the distance from the coordinate origin (0,0)
(top-left corner of the image),
θ is the line rotation angle in radians ( 0∼vertical line,π/2∼horizontal line ),
and votes is the value of accumulator.
```

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.hough_lines.DEFAULT_RHO
documentation: Distance resolution of the accumulator in pixels.

variables.name: cvlayer.cv.hough_lines.DEFAULT_THETA
documentation: Angle resolution of the accumulator in radians.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_QUALITY
documentation:
For JPEG, it can be a quality from 0 to 100 (the higher is the better).
Default value is 95.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_PROGRESSIVE
documentation: Enable JPEG features, 0 or 1, default is False.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_OPTIMIZE
documentation: Enable JPEG features, 0 or 1, default is False.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_RST_INTERVAL
documentation: JPEG restart interval, 0 - 65535, default is 0 - no restart.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_LUMA_QUALITY
documentation: Separate luma quality level, 0 - 100, default is -1 - don't use.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_CHROMA_QUALITY
documentation: Separate chroma quality level, 0 - 100, default is -1 - don't use.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.JPEG_SAMPLING_FACTOR
documentation: For JPEG, set sampling factor. See cv2.ImwriteJPEGSamplingFactorParams.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.PNG_COMPRESSION
documentation:
For PNG, it can be the compression level from 0 to 9.
A higher value means a smaller size and longer compression time.
If specified,
strategy is changed to IMWRITE_PNG_STRATEGY_DEFAULT (Z_DEFAULT_STRATEGY).
Default value is 1 (best speed setting).

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.PNG_STRATEGY
documentation: One of cv2.ImwritePNGFlags, default is IMWRITE_PNG_STRATEGY_RLE.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.PNG_BILEVEL
documentation: Binary level PNG, 0 or 1, default is 0.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.PXM_BINARY
documentation: For PPM, PGM, or PBM, it can be a binary format flag, 0 or 1. Default value is 1.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.WEBP_QUALITY
documentation:
Override EXR storage type (FLOAT (FP32) is default)

For WEBP, it can be a quality from 1 to 100 (the higher is the better).
By default (without any parameter) and for quality above 100 the lossless
compression is used.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.PAM_TUPLETYPE
documentation:
specify HDR compression

For PAM, sets the TUPLETYPE field to the corresponding string value that is defined
for the format

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.TIFF_RESUNIT
documentation:
For TIFF, use to specify which DPI resolution unit to set;
see libtiff documentation for valid values.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.TIFF_XDPI
documentation: For TIFF, use to specify the X direction DPI.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.TIFF_YDPI
documentation: For TIFF, use to specify the Y direction DPI.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImWriteFlags.TIFF_COMPRESSION
documentation:
For TIFF, use to specify the image compression scheme.
See libtiff for integer constants corresponding to compression formats.

Note,
for images whose depth is CV_32F, only libtiff's SGILOG compression scheme is used.
For other supported depths, the compression scheme can be specified by this flag;
LZW compression is the default.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.UNCHANGED
documentation:
If set, return the loaded image as is
(with alpha channel, otherwise it gets cropped).
Ignore EXIF orientation.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.GRAYSCALE
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the single channel grayscale image
(codec internal conversion).

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.COLOR
documentation: If set, always convert image to the 3 channel BGR color image.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.ANYDEPTH
documentation:
If set, return 16-bit/32-bit image when the input has the corresponding depth,
otherwise convert it to 8-bit.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.ANYCOLOR
documentation: If set, the image is read in any possible color format.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.LOAD_GDAL
documentation: If set, use the gdal driver for loading the image.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_GRAYSCALE_2
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the single channel grayscale image and
the image size reduced 1/2.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_COLOR_2
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the 3 channel BGR color image and
the image size reduced 1/2.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_GRAYSCALE_4
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the single channel grayscale image and
the image size reduced 1/4.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_COLOR_4
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the 3 channel BGR color image and the
image size reduced 1/4.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_GRAYSCALE_8
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the single channel grayscale image and
the image size reduced 1/8.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.REDUCED_COLOR_8
documentation:
If set, always convert image to the 3 channel BGR color image and
the image size reduced 1/8.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.image_io.ImReadModes.IGNORE_ORIENTATION
documentation: If set, do not rotate the image according to EXIF's orientation flag.

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractor.foreground_mask
documentation: Foreground mask as an 8-bit binary image

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractor.empty
documentation:
Returns true if the Algorithm is empty.
e.g. in the very beginning or after unsuccessful read.

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractor.apply
documentation:
Computes a foreground mask.

```
param image

Next video frame.

param learning_rate

The value between 0 and 1 that indicates
how fast the background model is learnt. Negative parameter value makes
the algorithm to use some automatically chosen learning rate.
0 means that the background model is not updated at all,
1 means that the background model is completely reinitialized from the
last frame.
```

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorKNN.dist2_threshold
documentation: Returns the threshold on the squared distance between the pixel and the sample.

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorKNN.history
documentation: Returns the number of last frames that affect the background model

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorKNN.knn_samples
documentation: Returns the number of neighbours, the k in the kNN

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorKNN.number_samples
documentation: Returns the number of data samples in the background model

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.background_ratio
documentation: Returns the "background ratio" parameter of the algorithm

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.history
documentation: Returns the number of last frames that affect the background model

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.number_mixtures
documentation: Returns the number of gaussian components in the background model

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.var_init
documentation: Returns the initial variance of each gaussian component

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.var_threshold
documentation: Returns the variance threshold for the pixel-model match

methods.name: cvlayer.cv.bgsub.BackgroundSubtractorMOG2.var_threshold_gen
documentation:
Returns the variance threshold for the pixel-model match used
for new mixture component generation.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.kmeans.KmeansFlags.RANDOM_CENTERS
documentation: Select random initial centers in each attempt.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.kmeans.KmeansFlags.PP_CENTERS
documentation: Use kmeans++ center initialization by Arthur and Vassilvitskii [Arthur2007].

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.kmeans.KmeansFlags.USE_INITIAL_LABELS
documentation:
During the first (and possibly the only) attempt,
use the user-supplied labels instead of computing them from the initial centers.
For the second and further attempts, use the random or semi-random centers.
Use one of `KMEANS_*_CENTERS` flag to specify the exact method.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.component.ConnectedComponentStatistics.x
documentation:
The leftmost (x) coordinate which is the inclusive start of the bounding box
in the horizontal direction.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.component.ConnectedComponentStatistics.y
documentation:
The topmost (y) coordinate which is the inclusive start of the bounding box
in the vertical direction.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.component.ConnectedComponentStatistics.width
documentation: The horizontal size of the bounding box.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.component.ConnectedComponentStatistics.height
documentation: The vertical size of the bounding box.

class_variables.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.component.ConnectedComponentStatistics.area
documentation: The total area (in pixels) of the connected component.

functions.name: cvlayer.cv.contour.moments.moments
documentation:
Only applicable to contour moments calculations from Python bindings.

Note that the numpy type for the input array should be either
`np.int32` or `np.float32`.

classes.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.SlopeInterceptForm
documentation:
Equation of a Straight Line

y = mx + b

classes.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.PointSlopeForm
documentation:
Point-Slope Equation of a Line

y − y1 = m(x − x1)

classes.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm

methods.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm.from_coord_polar
documentation:
m = tan(radians)
y - y1 = m (x - x1)
y = mx - mx1 + y1
0 = mx - mx1 + y1 - y
0 = mx - y + (-mx1 + y1)
A = m
B = -1
C = -m * x1 + y1

methods.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm.calc_x
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
Ax = -By -C
Ax = -(By + C)
x = -(By + C) / A

methods.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm.calc_y
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
By = -Ax -C
By = -(Ax + C)
y = -(Ax + C) / B

methods.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm.vertical_slope
documentation:
vertical_slope * slope = -1
vertical_slope = -1 / slope
vertical_slope = -1 / (-1 * (self.a / self.b))
vertical_slope = -1 * -1 * (self.b / self.a)
vertical_slope = self.b / self.a

methods.name: cvlayer.math.equation.linear.GeneralForm.intersection
documentation:
Ax + By + C = 0
Dx + Ey + F = 0
x = (CE - BF) / (BD - AE)
y = (CD - AF) / (AE - BD)

General Form of Equation of a Line

Ax + By + C = 0

documentation: Used when sign information must be maintained.

functions.name: cvlayer.math.angle.normalize_point
documentation: Move the center coordinate to the origin coordinate.

functions.name: cvlayer.math.angle.close_to_pivot
documentation: Make sure the angle presented is close to the pivot angle.

methods.name: cvlayer.rotate.protractor.Protractor.normalize_point
documentation: Move the center coordinate to the origin coordinate.

variables.name: cvlayer.chrono.datetime_filename.DATETIME_TO_DIRECTORY_STRFTIME_FORMAT
documentation:
"%H'
    Hour (24-hour clock) as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 23
'%M'
    Minute as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 59
'%S'
    Second as a zero-padded decimal number.
    e.g. 00, 01, …, 59
'%f'
    Microsecond as a decimal number, zero-padded to 6 digits.
    e.g. 000000, 000001, …, 999999
